1	import { EXERCISE_CATALOG } from '../../../data/exerciseCatalog';
2	import type { ResolvedExercise, WorkoutExercise } from '../types';
3	
4	// Merge a workout's exercise prescription with the catalog metadata. When the
5	// catalog has no matching entry (legacy import that couldn't be mapped), fall
6	// back to the inlined exercise_name and flag the result as unmapped so the UI
7	// can show an indicator and skip demo/technique chrome.
8	export function resolveExercise(entry: WorkoutExercise): ResolvedExercise {
9	  const cat = EXERCISE_CATALOG[entry.exercise_id];
10	  if (cat) {
11	    return {
12	      id: cat.id,
13	      name: cat.name,
14	      unmapped: false,
15	      sets: entry.sets,
16	      reps: entry.reps,
17	      rest: entry.rest,
18	      demoVideoId: cat.demoVideoId,
19	      demoVideoStart: cat.demoVideoStart,
20	      techniqueNote: cat.techniqueNote,
21	      usesWeight: cat.usesWeight,
22	      startingWeight: entry.starting_weight ?? cat.defaultStartingWeight,
23	      weightIncrement: entry.weight_increment ?? cat.defaultWeightIncrement,
24	    };
25	  }
26	  return {
27	    id: entry.exercise_id,
28	    name: entry.exercise_name ?? entry.exercise_id,
29	    unmapped: true,
30	    sets: entry.sets,
31	    reps: entry.reps,
32	    rest: entry.rest,
33	    usesWeight: entry.starting_weight != null,
34	    startingWeight: entry.starting_weight,
35	    weightIncrement: entry.weight_increment,
36	  };
37	}
38

Add equipment inventory store, Settings UI, and constrained weight picker

PR-B of the equipment-inventory feature. Adds a per-user inventory wrapped
in a LocationsState envelope (single 'Home' location for now, forward-compat
for multi-location work) and persists it in a new IDB 'inventory' store
(DB_VERSION bumped 2 → 3, additive migration).

Settings gains a new Equipment section: toggle which kinds you own, edit
per-kind weight chips with sensible presets (KB/DB/barbell), and add a
free-text note. The Workouts tab passes the active inventory through to
each ExerciseCard, which replaces the free-text weight input with a chip
strip when inventory is configured. The +/- buttons step through owned
weights only; drift entries (previously logged weight no longer owned)
remain rendered as a subtle border-only chip so historical data still
displays. When the user owns none of an exercise's equipment (and no
alternative group is satisfied), a one-line muted banner appears above
the card and the weight controls are dropped entirely.

suggestWeight gains an optional allowedWeights parameter that snaps the
suggestion DOWN to the highest owned weight ≤ the unconstrained value.

The export/import round-trips inventory: exportPayload includes
LocationsState when isInventoryConfigured returns true; applyImport
sanitizes (drops unknown EquipmentKind keys, coerces weights, dedups,
sorts) and saves via saveLocations. Absence of the field never wipes
existing inventory.

First-run users still see the free-input picker — the chip picker only
activates once any location has at least one owned kind.
EOF

gh pr create --reviewer patflynn --title "Add equipment inventory store, Settings UI, and constrained weight picker" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary

PR-B of three landing the equipment-inventory feature. PR-A added the app-level equipment catalog and exercise cross-reference; PR-C will integrate the AI program planner with the inventory.

This PR adds the user-facing storage, editor, and weight-picker constraints:

- **Per-user inventory store** wrapped in a `LocationsState` envelope (single 'Home' location now; multi-location is future-compat). Persisted in a new IDB `inventory` store; DB_VERSION bumped 2 → 3 (additive migration).
- **Settings → Equipment section** — toggle which kinds you own, edit per-kind weight chips with sensible presets (KB/DB/barbell), free-text note.
- **Constrained weight picker** on each `ExerciseCard`: when inventory is configured, the free-input is replaced with a horizontal chip strip over owned weights. `+ / −` step through owned weights only. Previously-logged weights that are no longer owned still render as drift chips (border-only) so historical data displays.
- **Drift banner** above each card when the user owns none of the required equipment (and no alternative group is satisfied). Weight controls drop entirely in that case.
- **`suggestWeight`** gains an optional `allowedWeights` param — snaps the suggestion DOWN to the highest owned weight ≤ the unconstrained value.
- **Export / import** round-trips inventory: present in the payload only when configured; sanitized on import (unknown EquipmentKind keys dropped, weights deduped/sorted); absence never wipes existing inventory.
- **First-run** users keep the existing free-input picker until they configure something — regression-safe for existing users.

## Test plan

- [x] Unit tests pass (`allowedWeightsForExercise`, `isExerciseSupportedByInventory` covering bodyweight sentinel + alternatives logic)
- [x] e2e tests pass
- [x] First-run users still see the free-input picker
- [x] Configured users see the chip picker
- [x] Drift banner appears when required equipment is removed
- [x] Drift chips render for historical log entries whose weight is no longer owned
- [x] Export / import round-trips inventory

Run: 20260529-1605-b2e31704
EOF
)" 2>&1 | tail -10

content.text:
PR opened: https://github.com/gunk-dev/flux/pull/85

Summary of what landed in this PR-B:

- **Inventory storage** — `src/data/inventory.ts` with `LocationsState` envelope; `src/tabs/Workouts/state.ts` bumped DB to v3 with new `inventory` store, plus sanitizing `loadLocations` / `saveLocations`, and `clearAll` extended.
- **Equipment resolver** — `src/tabs/Workouts/logic/equipmentResolve.ts` with `allowedWeightsForExercise`, `isExerciseSupportedByInventory`, `missingKindsForExercise` (honors `equipmentAlternatives`, treats bodyweight as always owned).
- **Settings UI** — new Equipment section in `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` grouped by category, with toggle / chip editor / presets (KB/DB/barbell) / note.
- **Picker** — `ExerciseCard.tsx` renders a chip strip with drift styling when inventory is configured; `+ / −` steps through owned weights; weight controls hide when nothing is owned. `progression.ts` `suggestWeight` snaps down to owned weights.
- **Drift banner** above each card when configured but unsupported.
- **Export/import** — `ExportPayload.locations` round-trips with sanitization; absence preserves existing inventory.
- **Tests** — `tests/inventory.spec.ts` covers unit (resolver + alternatives + bodyweight) and e2e (golden path, drift on removed weight, missing-equipment banner, first-run fallback, round-trip). All 36 tests pass; `klaus _pre-review` clean.
